goal: wave5-harvest: build .org Harvest Hub — stories, prompts, moderation
summary: Wave 5 Harvest Hub: story submission + listing, weekly prompt replies, and admin moderation on .org
acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes the Harvest Hub story + prompt + moderation API: POST /api/stories (auth), GET /api/stories (public published list), GET /api/stories/moderation (admin), PATCH /api/stories/:id (admin), GET /api/prompts/current (public), POST /api/prompts/:id/replies (auth); all inputs land in the contributions table with the correct source enum

Login is required to post stories and prompt replies (401 when anonymous); moderation endpoints require an admin session gated by ADMIN_EMAILS (401 anon / 403 non-admin); a prompts table is seeded idempotently on startup
ai-transformation.org has working /stories (published list + submit), /prompts (current prompt + reply), and an admin-only moderation view that can publish/feature/archive/spam pending stories
All workspace packages typecheck, both Next apps build, backend harvest tests pass, and the wave ships as a single draft PR to main

tasks.name: backend-harvest
tasks.scopedGoal:
Build the Wave 5 Harvest Hub backend for the ai-transformation pnpm+turbo monorepo (Hono backend, better-sqlite3, Zod shared types). This is the ONLY task touching packages/shared and apps/backend. Do NOT touch apps/web-io, apps/web-org, or apps/combined. Study the existing code first: apps/backend/src/index.ts (route mounting + CORS + sessionMiddleware), apps/backend/src/lanes/harvest/index.ts (existing POST /inquiries), apps/backend/src/db/index.ts (better-sqlite3 module, migrations, insertContribution, session/user helpers), apps/backend/src/middleware/session.ts and src/types/session.ts (c.get('user') is UserRow|null), apps/backend/src/lanes/auth/auth.test.ts (vitest pattern using app.fetch with cookies), and packages/shared/src/index.ts (existing exports — KEEP THEM ALL).

Build EXACTLY to this finalized contract so the parallel frontend slice can integrate without coupling.

AUTH MODEL:
- Stories and prompt replies REQUIRE a logged-in session (cookie atx_session resolved by sessionMiddleware into c.get('user')). Anonymous -> HTTP 401 { ok:false, error }.
- Moderation endpoints require an ADMIN session: logged in AND user.email is in ADMIN_EMAILS (comma-separated env var, compared case-insensitively after trim). Anonymous -> 401; logged-in non-admin -> 403. If ADMIN_EMAILS is unset/empty, nobody is admin. Put an isAdmin(user) helper in the harvest lane (do not edit other lanes).

ENDPOINTS (add to apps/backend/src/lanes/harvest/index.ts; it is mounted under /api so public paths are /api/...). KEEP the existing POST /inquiries handler unchanged.
1) POST /api/stories (auth required): body { title: string 4..160, body: string 50..8000, name?: string <=120 }. Insert a contributions row: source='web_story', status='new', subject=title, body=body, site='org', email=user.email, name = payload.name ?? user.name ?? null, user_id=user.id, metadata='{}', id=crypto.randomUUID(), created_at=new Date().toISOString(). Return 201 { ok:true, id }. 400 on invalid body, 401 if not logged in.
2) GET /api/stories (public): return published stories where status IN ('published','featured'), newest first (created_at DESC). Response { ok:true, stories: Array<{ id, title, body, name, publishedSlug, createdAt, featured }> } where title=subject, name=author display name, publishedSlug=published_slug, featured = (status==='featured').
3) GET /api/stories/moderation (admin): return ALL contributions where source='web_story', newest first, each { id, title, body, name, email, status, publishedSlug, createdAt }. 401 anon, 403 non-admin.
4) PATCH /api/stories/:id (admin): body { status: one of 'reviewed'|'published'|'featured'|'archived'|'spam', publishedSlug?: string }. The target row must exist AND have source='web_story' (else 404). Update status; set reviewed_at=now and reviewed_by=user.id; when transitioning to 'published' or 'featured' and no published_slug is set, auto-generate a url-safe slug from the title plus a short unique suffix; if publishedSlug is provided use it. Return 200 { ok:true, story: { id, title, body, name, email, status, publishedSlug, createdAt } }. 400 invalid status, 401 anon, 403 non-admin, 404 not found.
5) GET /api/prompts/current (public): return the active weekly prompt. Response { ok:true, prompt: { id, question, weekOf } | null }.
6) POST /api/prompts/:id/replies (auth required): body { body: string 10..5000 }. The prompt id must exist (else 404). Insert a contributions row: source='web_prompt_reply', status='new', body=payload.body, site='org', email=user.email, name=user.name ?? null, user_id=user.id, metadata=JSON.stringify({ promptId: id }), id=uuid, created_at=now. Return 201 { ok:true, id }. 400 invalid, 401 anon, 404 unknown prompt.

DB (apps/backend/src/db/index.ts): KEEP all existing exports/migrations. Add a prompts table to runMigrations: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS prompts ( id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, question TEXT NOT NULL, week_of TEXT, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active', created_at TEXT NOT NULL ). Seed it idempotently with INSERT OR IGNORE for 2 fixed-id prompts (e.g. id 'prompt-2026-w24' and 'prompt-2026-w25' with realistic AI-transformation community questions and week_of dates). Add and export helpers: listPublishedStories(), listStoriesForModeration(), getContributionById(id), updateContributionModeration({ id, status, publishedSlug, reviewedBy, reviewedAt }), getCurrentPrompt() (most recent active by week_of/created_at DESC), getPromptById(id). Use parameterized statements like the existing code.

packages/shared/src/index.ts: KEEP every existing export. ADD Zod schemas + inferred types and EXTEND the object returned by createApiClient (do not remove existing methods): storyPayloadSchema, storyStatusSchema (enum of the 5 statuses above plus 'new'), storySchema (public), storyModerationSchema, storiesResponseSchema, promptSchema, currentPromptResponseSchema, promptReplyPayloadSchema, storyModerationListResponseSchema, storyModerationResponseSchema. Add client methods submitStory(payload), getStories(), getCurrentPrompt(), submitPromptReply(promptId, payload), getStoriesForModeration(), moderateStory(id, body) (the auth'd ones must pass credentials:'include'). The frontend slice will use plain fetch, but ship these for completeness and the client tests.

.env.example: add ADMIN_EMAILS= under a 'Wave 5 harvest' comment.

Update apps/backend/src/lanes/harvest/INTERFACE.md Provides section to reflect the implemented routes and the ADMIN_EMAILS gate. Add fixtures data/simulators/harvest/story.json and data/simulators/harvest/prompt-reply.json.

TESTS: add apps/backend/src/lanes/harvest/harvest.test.ts (vitest), mirroring auth.test.ts setup (temp SQLITE_PATH per test, closeDbForTests in afterEach). Cover: anonymous POST /api/stories -> 401; create a user+session via the exported db helpers (upsertUserByGoogle + createSession) and POST with cookie atx_session=<id> -> 201; GET /api/stories returns only published/featured; admin (ADMIN_EMAILS set to that user's email) GET /api/stories/moderation -> 200 list incl the new story; non-admin -> 403; PATCH /api/stories/:id status='published' -> 200 and the story then appears in GET /api/stories; GET /api/prompts/current returns a seeded prompt; authed POST /api/prompts/:id/replies -> 201 and an unknown prompt -> 404; invalid bodies -> 400. Set NODE_ENV=test so the server does not auto-listen.

GATES (run from repo root): pnpm install; pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/shared typecheck; pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend typecheck; pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test. Also smoke-run the server: SQLITE_PATH=/tmp/wave5-be.db ADMIN_EMAILS=admin@example.com PORT=3001 pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend dev, then curl GET /api/prompts/current (expect a prompt) and confirm GET /api/health still returns ok, then stop it. Keep GET /api/health and CORS intact; lane logic stays in the lane file, index.ts only mounts new routes if needed (existing app.route('/api', harvestRouter) already covers the new paths).
acceptance: POST /api/stories requires auth (401 anon) and on a valid authed request persists a contributions row source='web_story' status='new' returning 201 {ok:true,id}; invalid body returns 400

GET /api/stories returns only published/featured stories; GET /api/stories/moderation and PATCH /api/stories/:id are admin-gated via ADMIN_EMAILS (401 anon, 403 non-admin) and PATCH publishing makes a story appear in GET /api/stories
GET /api/prompts/current returns a seeded active prompt and POST /api/prompts/:id/replies (auth) persists source='web_prompt_reply' with metadata.promptId (404 on unknown prompt, 401 anon)
packages/shared keeps all existing exports and adds the new harvest schemas + createApiClient methods; a prompts table is created and seeded idempotently
pnpm typecheck (shared + backend) passes, pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test passes (new harvest.test.ts), and GET /api/health still returns ok
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- From repo root: `pnpm install`.

## Automated
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/shared typecheck`
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend typecheck`
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` (must include the new harvest.test.ts and pass)

## Manual
- Start backend: `SQLITE_PATH=/tmp/wave5-be.db ADMIN_EMAILS=admin@example.com PORT=3001 pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend dev`.
- `curl -s localhost:3001/api/prompts/current` -> {ok:true,prompt:{...}}.
- `curl -i -X POST localhost:3001/api/stories -H 'content-type: application/json' -d '{"title":"My rollout","body":"...50+ chars..."}'` with NO cookie -> 401.
- `curl -s localhost:3001/api/health` -> {ok:true,...}.

## Gotchas
- Keep POST /inquiries, GET /api/health, CORS, and all existing shared exports intact. Stories/prompt-replies are login-gated; moderation is ADMIN_EMAILS-gated. Lane logic stays in the harvest lane file.

tasks.name: web-org-harvest
tasks.scopedGoal:
Build the Wave 5 Harvest Hub UI on the ai-transformation.org Next.js App Router frontend (React 19, Tailwind v4). This task ONLY touches apps/web-org. Do NOT modify packages/shared, apps/backend, apps/web-io, or apps/combined, and do NOT import any new exports from @ai-transformation/shared — use plain fetch against the finalized contract below. Study existing patterns first: apps/web-org/components/inquiry-form.tsx (client form pattern: validation, submitting/success/error states, var(--*) styling, rounded-3xl card), apps/web-org/components/auth-nav.tsx (how it reads /api/auth/me with credentials:'include'), apps/web-org/components/site-chrome.tsx (nav already links /stories and /prompts), app/globals.css (CSS variables: --muted, --accent, --border, --card, --background, --foreground), and the existing placeholder pages app/stories/page.tsx, app/stories/submit/page.tsx, app/prompts/page.tsx.

Endpoint resolution everywhere: const API_BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL ?? ''; then fetch(`${API_BASE}/api/...`). In production this is a relative path routed by the combined proxy to the backend; local dev can override the base. All authed/credentialed requests MUST pass credentials:'include'.

FINALIZED CONTRACT (build to this exactly; the backend slice implements it):
- POST /api/stories (login required): JSON { title: string 4..160, body: string 50..8000, name?: string <=120 }. 201 { ok:true, id }; 400 { ok:false, error }; 401 when not logged in.
- GET /api/stories (public): { ok:true, stories: Array<{ id, title, body, name, publishedSlug, createdAt, featured }> }.
- GET /api/auth/me (existing): { ok:true, user: { id,email,name,picture,createdAt } | null } — use to detect login + admin gating UI.
- GET /api/stories/moderation (admin): { ok:true, stories: Array<{ id, title, body, name, email, status, publishedSlug, createdAt }> }; 401/403 when not admin.
- PATCH /api/stories/:id (admin): JSON { status: 'reviewed'|'published'|'featured'|'archived'|'spam', publishedSlug?: string } -> { ok:true, story:{...} }.
- GET /api/prompts/current (public): { ok:true, prompt: { id, question, weekOf } | null }.
- POST /api/prompts/:id/replies (login required): JSON { body: string 10..5000 } -> 201 { ok:true, id }; 401 when not logged in.

DELIVERABLES (use the warmer .org community tone throughout; match existing .org styling and the editorial/serif-title aesthetic):
1) app/stories/page.tsx: keep it a server component exporting metadata, rendering a NEW client component components/story-list.tsx that fetches GET /api/stories on mount and renders the published stories (title, author name, body excerpt or full, a 'Featured' badge when featured). Show a graceful empty state ('No stories published yet — be the first to share.') and an error state. Include a clear link/CTA to /stories/submit.
2) app/stories/submit/page.tsx: server component with metadata rendering a NEW client component components/story-form.tsx ('use client'). On mount check GET /api/auth/me; if not logged in, show a friendly 'Sign in to share your story' prompt linking to `${API_BASE}/api/auth/google?return=/stories/submit`. If logged in, render the form (title, body textarea, optional display name) with client validation mirroring the contract (title 4..160, body 50..8000, name <=120), submitting/success/error states, and a success confirmation explaining the story will be reviewed before publishing.
3) app/prompts/page.tsx: server component with metadata rendering a NEW client component components/prompt-reply.tsx that fetches GET /api/prompts/current, shows the current question + weekOf, and (when logged in) a reply form posting { body } to POST /api/prompts/:id/replies (validation 10..5000). When logged out, show the prompt plus a 'Sign in to reply' link to `${API_BASE}/api/auth/google?return=/prompts`. Handle the prompt:null empty state.
4) NEW app/moderation/page.tsx: server component with metadata rendering a NEW client component components/moderation-panel.tsx ('use client'). On mount fetch GET /api/auth/me then GET /api/stories/moderation with credentials:'include'. If the moderation fetch returns 401/403, show 'You do not have moderation access.' (do not crash). Otherwise render the review queue grouped/labelled by status, each story showing title/author/email/body/status, with action buttons that PATCH /api/stories/:id to publish, feature, archive, or mark spam, optimistically refreshing the list after a successful PATCH. Add a 'Moderation' nav link in components/site-chrome.tsx (it can always be visible; the page self-gates).

GATES (from repo root): pnpm install; pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org typecheck; pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org build. Both must pass. (web-io and shared/backend are untouched.)
acceptance: /stories renders a client-fetched list of published stories (GET /api/stories) with empty + error states and a link to /stories/submit

/stories/submit gates on GET /api/auth/me: signed-out users get a sign-in prompt; signed-in users get a validated story form that POSTs to /api/stories with credentials and shows submitting/success/error states
/prompts shows the current prompt (GET /api/prompts/current) and a login-gated reply form posting to /api/prompts/:id/replies
/moderation self-gates (handles 401/403 gracefully) and lets an admin publish/feature/archive/spam stories via PATCH /api/stories/:id, refreshing the queue; a Moderation nav link is added
pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org typecheck and build both pass; packages/shared, apps/backend, apps/web-io, apps/combined untouched; no new @ai-transformation/shared imports
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- From repo root: `pnpm install`.

## Automated
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org typecheck`
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org build`

## Manual
- Run the web-org dev server and open /stories, /stories/submit, /prompts, /moderation; confirm pages render, signed-out gating shows sign-in prompts, client validation blocks bad input, and submit issues the correct POST/PATCH (verify in devtools network tab; backend need not run for the UI check).

## Gotchas
- New form/list/panel components must be Client Components ('use client'); page.tsx files stay server components for metadata. Use plain fetch with credentials:'include' for authed calls; do not import new exports from @ai-transformation/shared. Endpoint base = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL ?? ''.

tasks.name: merge-harvest
tasks.scopedGoal:
Integration + ship task for the Wave 5 Harvest Hub. Your clone starts from the backend slice branch (orch/wave5-harvest/backend-harvest). Merge the web-org slice branch (orch/wave5-harvest/web-org-harvest) into it. The two slices touch disjoint paths (packages/shared + apps/backend + data/simulators/harvest vs apps/web-org), so conflicts are unlikely; if any conflict arises, keep BOTH sides' changes.

After merging, from repo root run `pnpm install`, then verify the whole wave end to end:
- Typecheck all four packages: `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/shared typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org typecheck`.
- Run backend tests: `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test`.
- Build both web apps: `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io build` and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org build`.
- Start the backend (SQLITE_PATH=/tmp/wave5-merge.db ADMIN_EMAILS=admin@example.com PORT=3001) and exercise the live API: GET /api/prompts/current returns a prompt; anonymous POST /api/stories -> 401; seed a user+session by running a tiny node/tsx one-off that imports the built/dev db helpers (upsertUserByGoogle + createSession) against the SAME SQLITE_PATH, then curl POST /api/stories with cookie atx_session=<id> -> 201; GET /api/stories shows nothing until you PATCH that story to status='published' (admin cookie + ADMIN_EMAILS includes that user's email) and then it appears; POST /api/prompts/<seeded-id>/replies (authed) -> 201; GET /api/health still ok. If seeding a session is impractical in the harness, rely on the backend vitest suite (pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test) as the authoritative auth-path evidence and still curl the public endpoints.

Then open a DRAFT pull request to main (base main) summarizing Wave 5 Harvest Hub: story submission + published listing, weekly prompt replies, admin moderation (ADMIN_EMAILS), and the prompts table. List the new endpoints and the login/admin gating. Do not merge or close the PR. Keep the .orchestrate/ directory out of the PR diff.
acceptance: The web-org slice is merged with the backend slice so the branch contains both backend + frontend Harvest Hub changes
All four packages typecheck, backend tests pass, and both web apps build successfully

Live API check: GET /api/prompts/current ok; anonymous POST /api/stories -> 401; an authed story can be created, published via PATCH, and then appears in GET /api/stories; GET /api/health still ok (or vitest suite stands in as auth-path evidence)
A draft PR to main is opened summarizing the Wave 5 Harvest Hub
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- Repo contains both slices after the merge. `pnpm install` from repo root.

## Automated
- Typecheck shared, backend, web-io, web-org.
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test`.
- Build web-io and web-org.

## Manual
- Start backend (SQLITE_PATH=/tmp/wave5-merge.db ADMIN_EMAILS=admin@example.com PORT=3001). GET /api/prompts/current -> prompt; anonymous POST /api/stories -> 401; authed create + PATCH publish -> appears in GET /api/stories; GET /api/health -> ok.

## Gotchas
- Disjoint paths; prefer keeping both sides on any unexpected conflict. Keep .orchestrate/ out of the PR diff.

tasks.name: verify-harvest
tasks.scopedGoal: Independently verify the integrated Wave 5 Harvest Hub on the merged branch. Actually run the checks — do not pattern-match. From repo root: `pnpm install`; typecheck shared, backend, web-io, web-org; `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test`; build web-io and web-org. Then start the backend (SQLITE_PATH=/tmp/wave5-verify.db ADMIN_EMAILS=admin@example.com PORT=3001) and confirm: GET /api/prompts/current returns a seeded prompt; anonymous POST /api/stories returns 401; GET /api/health still returns ok; GET /api/stories returns a well-formed { ok:true, stories: [...] } payload. For the authenticated paths, prefer the backend vitest suite as evidence (confirm it covers story create -> 401 anon, authed 201, GET moderation admin/403, PATCH publish makes the story public, prompt reply 201 + 404 unknown). Also confirm apps/web-org has /stories, /stories/submit, /prompts, and /moderation pages wired to the correct endpoints with login/admin gating, and that they build. Report a Verification verdict with concrete execution evidence (commands run + outcomes) for every merge-harvest acceptance criterion.
acceptance: Verification section includes concrete execution evidence (commands run + outcomes) for every merge-harvest acceptance criterion
